title: begonia cauliflora
intro: Begonia cauliflora, also known as the Begonia with axillary flowers or the tuberous Begonia, is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Begonia cauliflora include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is also used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, thanks to its expectorant and diuretic properties. Traditionally, Begonia cauliflora is prepared as a tea or tincture to be used in herbal medicine, and its leaves can be used in infusions to promote healing and relaxation.
